data.title: Judge Declines to Order DOJ to Review Records in Roman Storm Case
data.description:
The federal judge overseeing Roman Storm's prosecution declined to order the Department of Justice to review its records for any materials it might have missed that would help the Tornado Cash developer at the end of a 30-minute hearing Friday morning, though she told the government it should not have any disclosure issues.Judge Katherine Polk Failla also ruled that there were no Brady violation concerns with the Department of Justice's conversations with the Financial Crimes Enforcement Network (FinCEN) about whether mixers needed to register as money transmitters — the conversation that prosecutors pursuing Samourai Wallet developers had with FinCEN officials, but not the prosecutors on Storm's case — one of the DOJ representatives said in the phone conference on Friday.If the judge had found that prosecutors had withheld information, it could affect the case moving forward."I'm not going to require a further review based on the representations made that there's no additional material of this type, and based on my views that I don't believe the material was exculpatory," she said
source: https://www.coindesk.com/policy/2025/05/30/judge-declines-to-order-doj-to-review-records-in-roman-storm-case

data.title: Two NYPD Allegedly Officers Caught in Crypto Kidnapping Storm, Duties Curtailed
data.description:
Two detectives have faced modified duty as NYPD inspectors examine claims they moonlighted for alleged crypto criminals who kidnapped and tortured an Italian businessman in a SoHo apartment. One officer reportedly drove the victim from the airport before the weeks-long ordeal began.
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/two-nypd-allegedly-officers-caught-in-crypto-kidnapping-storm-duties-curtailed/

data.title: Sui Approves Return of $162M in Frozen Exploit Funds
data.description:
TL;DR Sui approved the return of $162 million to those affected by the Cetus exploit, which took place on May 22 and resulted in losses exceeding $220 million. The frozen funds will be transferred to a multisig wallet and distributed under a plan that includes resources from Cetus and an emergency loan from the Sui. . . 
source: https://crypto-economy.com/sui-approves-return-of-162m-in-frozen-exploit-funds/
